reviews.title: Astounding that this place has gone down-hill so quickly.
reviews.text: The pandemic seems to have really affected the friendliness and level of service this hotel provides. It used to be my go to business Hotel for my many business trips to Bangkok but not anymore. The Club Lounge offering has dropped off a cliff. The Pool Area needs updating, No in-room info (just a QR code - what?). No amenities in Bathroom, I asked for a razor, they said they would bring 2, only one appeared. Do not mention the check-in. It was very unfriendly and basically a hotel has a up-hill struggle after that. I am bitterly disappointed because anyone who conducts a lot f business travel will know how great it is to have a reliable choice in each country. Not 5* service and very little in the way of smiles. So sad.
reviews.response:
Dear Neil,

As already indicated in a separate correspondence, I hope you will accept our sincere apologies for any inconvenience we may have caused. 

Guest feedback is the most important of all; therefore, we would like to extend our appreciation for taking the time to comment on each aspect of your stay through various channels. We hope to make our guests feel special and cared for each day, starting from the moment you arrive and unfortunately, it was not the same in your case this time.

Once again, we thank you for the constructive feedback and will always endeavor to improve our service standards. 

Sincerely,
Kalpana Khadka

reviews.title: Disappointing Experience
reviews.text:
This was my first stay in Sofitel Sukhumvit, and I can only say how disappointed I was with the level of service and professionalism that was displayed throughout my stay. 

First and foremost, the standard of housekeeping needs to be reassessed for such a premium establishment. I was dismayed to find ashes still littered on the coffee table that I smoked on and even the carpets, which was distinct from doorway after returning from a lunch appointment. The executive housekeeper reassured me that it was a one off incident and apologized profusely with a complimentary set of macaroons. Later that evening, I called for housekeeping services again before I stepped out for dinner. Upon my return, I was in utter shock! I found the laundry bags with the dirty box strewn all over my bed, and my sleeping clothes which I left on the bed littered on the floor. Thinking that my room may have been broken into, I called the security and night manager which promised and investigation. The night manager, Ploy was kind enough to update me early the next morning before her shift ended. Apparently they checked the records and it was a housekeeper who last accessed my room!

And that was not the end of my ordeal in Sofitel Sukhumvit. I went up to the Club Millesime after a last minute decision to extend my stay for another 2 nights. The counter was manned by 2 of your employees who was trying to strongly adamant of me staying within the same suite, even when i made a prior request for a lower class of room due to budget constraints. While one was understanding enough, the other club employee threw me a disgusted stare, snorted and stomped off. Is this the elitist culture that Sofitel prides itself? Are guests treated in a different manner based on financial statuses? I am not a difficult guest to deal with; all I expect is the basic respect and level of service that should be availed to all guests alike.

I certainly hope that the hotel management would look into this and take appropri

The club decor is a bit dated, there were not enough staff on duty and the drinks and food ran out. Having stayed at a Sofitel Club room in Macau a few years ago  and being delighted we were hoping for a good experience at Bangkok Sukhumvit. We had also booked a club room for 1 night on our return to Bangkok in 2 weeks time. We cancelled it and will be staying at a different hotel club room. The problem seems to be a lack of management oversight  and we did not want to stay at this hotel again.

reviews.title: Complaint Regarding Fire Drill Disruption on 24th September 2025
reviews.text:
Here’s a professional draft you can use for your complaint to Sofitel Bangkok:

⸻

Subject: Complaint Regarding Fire Drill Disruption on 24th September 2025

Dear Sofitel Bangkok Management,

I am writing to express my dissatisfaction regarding my recent stay at your hotel on 24th September 2025.

According to the notice provided in the room, the scheduled fire drill alarm was stated to last for 20–30 seconds. However, during the drill, the alarm rang for a significantly longer duration. This caused unnecessary disruption to my schedule. At the time, I was in the middle of an important meeting and was unable to proceed due to the prolonged noise.

The inaccurate information provided created inconvenience and frustration. As a guest, I rely on the information given by the hotel to plan my day effectively, and in this case, it was misleading.

I kindly request that the hotel reviews its communication and ensures that future notices reflect accurate information, so that guests can be better prepared. I would also appreciate an acknowledgment of this complaint and any steps taken to address this matter.

Thank you for your attention, and I look forward to your response.

Sincerely,
Edwin

reviews.title: Great but then not so great
reviews.text:
The hotel is wonderfully located if you would like to enjoy the nightlife and restaurants, the staff service and attitude ranged from good to very good.  The sales pitch to upgrade to a higher level room was a bit over the top and historically was just offered for free not sold to us.  I must say we were a little surprised as the Agoda rating of 9 in a 5 star hotel normally means the hotel is excellent, but it did not seem so to us.
The room was big enough but being on the 26th floor we noticed a constant noise caused by the wind swirling around the building, it was not loud enough to drown out the air conditioning system so it was not a big problem.  The big problem was/is the drainage system which does not work well, a constant smell in the bathroom especially during peak times.  I asked the front office staff to have house keeping try to clean the drain but even though they tried there was not a lot that could be done about it.  I am guessing this is a much bigger problem than one room and it must be difficult for the staff facing this problem from the guests.
The bed is comfortable, the gym is very good the breakfast is great and with the different food bars spaced out around the entire floor area there was no or little queuing which was good as it can get very busy, the staff do a good job managing so many guests.
All in all the hotel is worthwhile but it is not an Agoda five star level 9 rating.

reviews.title: Short Break in Sukhumvit
reviews.text:
Was excited to stay here as I've heard a lot of good things about the Sofitel Sukhumvit.
Previously we've stayed at the Hyatt and the Westin on the same stretch that are on the same Grade as the Sofitel.
Unfortunately the property didn't meet my expectations.
Place was Clean and Comfortable, but lacked the amenities that was expected from this level of Hotel.

Pros :
Location, Bedding, Room Size

Cons :
In room Tea/Coffee Making facilities was very very ordinary, I was shocked to see Nescafe sachets instead of the usual Coffee Pods or a French Press
Breakfast Buffet also lacked variety in comparison with the Hyatt
Reception staff were quite preoccupied and was quite inefficient
The Guest services tel line would ring and ring and ring with no answer
On my last day, the concierge was quite obnoxious on trying to get me a taxi. Eventually I used Grab and booked my own while he was pottering around trying to flag one down from the street. I found this amusing as most toehr concierge officers have their Taxis on speed dial.

Anyhow. Overall pleasant, but I SL wouldn't stay again

Overall, the customer care at this hotel is outstanding, and I highly recommend it to anyone looking for a top-notch stay. Thank you, Sofitel, for a wonderful experience!

reviews.title: Constant Noise Issue
reviews.text:
I was placed in a room that was right across from the BTS sky train and so couldn't sleep well bc of the noise from the train passing by. Then I moved to a different room but the noise from the concert that was happening in the night market across from the hotel was so loud during the day and night. (During the day, they were doing sound checks but they weren't sound checks. Full blown sounds.) I called the front desk to ask about the noise and they said the concert was happening throughout the days I was staying at the hotel. I FEEL LIKE THEY SHOULD INFORM ABOUT THIS KIND OF INFO OR OFFER DISCOUNT BC I DID NOT FEEL RESTED AT ALL DURING MY STAY.
But the restaurant staffs were very profesional and considerate, while the reception check in and out staff really should speak better English and have better attitude. Please make the guy who helps out with luggage during check out to work at the reception desk. He had the best attitude, smile and willingness to be helpful. Reception staff really not at the level of 5 stat hotel.
reviews.response:
Dear SongYi,

Thank you for sharing your detailed feedback with us. We are truly sorry to learn how much the external noise affected your rest during your stay, both from the nearby BTS line and from the events taking place in the surrounding area.

We would like to clarify, with transparency, that the concert activity at the food market was not known to the hotel in advance. As this was an external event outside of our control and without prior notification to us, we regret that we were unable to inform you beforehand. Nevertheless, we fully understand how disruptive this must have felt, and we sincerely apologise for the discomfort it caused you.

We are also sorry that even after the room move, you were still unable to feel properly rested. While we strive to minimise disturbances whenever possible, we recognise that the overall experience did not meet your expectations in this regard.

Thank you as well for acknowledging the professionalism and care of our restaurant team and the c

reviews.title: Wasn't Special
reviews.text: 5 Star French hotel.  I expected more.  The pool is small and shaded by the hotel for most of the day.  Everything is very expensive.  The coffee shop, the pool drinks, the rooftop bar/restaurant.   4 regular sized meatball entree was $20.  There were no complimentary snacks or drinks in the room.  At Grand Centre Point Terminal 21, they gave you snacks, cold bottled water and Coke, at no charge each day.  The room was plush.  While the marble bathroom was luxurious, the toilet was in a small cramped space.  Few hangers.  Certainly not enough for 2 people, let alone 1.  The high back chair could use an ottoman.  On a good note, the a/c didn't make noise and the TV speakers were really loud.  I was also able to sinc my iPad and Android phone to the TV to play videos.  Using the in-room table to control everything from all lights to the temperature to TV controls, was interesting.  But, there were times I didn't want to get the tablet to turn on the lights in the bathroom.  The bed was comfortable and housekeeping was good.  There's just so much better value from other hotel choices nearby.  I will not go back.

I do not recommend this hotel for families with kids .. would never expect to see such scene right in front of this hotel.. it looks just like a mini redlight district..

reviews.title: Swimming pool renovation
reviews.text: The location was good, the cleanliness of the accommodation was fine, and the attitude of the staff was not bad. It is true that Sofitel provides turn-down service twice a day, which is very convenient. However, without any prior notice, the pool was under construction and unavailable; even though I had arranged for a hotel 20 minutes away by car, the shuttle could only be used at specific times, which was very inconvenient. During my 5-night stay, I only took the shuttle once and ended up taking a Grab back to the accommodation while wet. I had breakfast every day for 5 days, but the taste and variety were lacking... there was nothing appealing. This is the part I regret the most.
reviews.response:
Dear HYEMI,

Thank you for taking the time to share your review. We are pleased to know that our location, cleanliness, staff attitude, and turndown service were positive parts of your stay. At the same time, please accept our sincere apologies for the disappointment caused by the pool renovation and the inconvenience of using the alternative pool arrangement. We understand that a fixed shuttle schedule cannot fully replace the ease of having the hotel pool available, and we regret that this affected your comfort.

We are also sorry that breakfast did not meet your expectations in taste and variety. Your comments have been shared with the relevant teams, as clear communication around facility changes and a satisfying dining experience are both important areas where we must continue to improve.

We appreciate your candid feedback and hope to have the opportunity to provide you with a more complete and comfortable experience in the future.

Warmest regards,
Krit T.
Guest Experience Manag

reviews.title: Soaked from the evening downpour without any cover.
reviews.text: Use after 5 years for 7 nights. Overall: The hotel was renovated in 2023, and the linens were new and comfortable. I stayed on the 29th floor, and there was a very kind housekeeping lady who made my stay pleasant. The room had some changes like the desk and sofa, but it was neither good nor bad. All requests and room controls were done through a tablet, making responses quick, but sometimes the details of my requests were not met, and there were often shortages. I thought it would be even better if the control panel allowed for curtain opening and closing. Amenities: All amenities, including shampoo and conditioner, were in refillable bottles. Drinking water was also provided in bottles. The toothbrushes were wooden. The hotel is environmentally conscious. Location: The skywalk has been extended, connecting to the 3rd floor of the hotel, so even during moderate evening downpours, you won’t get wet when using the BTS from Asoke station. I felt this was a major advantage. Breakfast: The weekend rush hour starts after 9:00 AM, and there was a wait for seats. The variety of the menu remained rich, unchanged from before the renovation. Gym area: The cleanliness has been maintained as it was before the renovation. In the past, they provided zip-lock bags in the lockers for wet swimsuits, but that was unfortunately discontinued.

reviews.title: Good but..
reviews.text:
Good experience although rather pricey. The room was spacious, uniquely designed and ultra clean. The staff are incredibly helpful and friendly. Welcome drink of lemongrass/pandan was very delicious. Breakfast was included for my deal but I would not pay extra for it as it's very expensive at 1400++ bhat. This hotel is very close to Terminal 21 mall and the train station for added convenience.

The room is mostly controlled via the tablet. ie TV, AC, etc... This is very unique and fancy but I'm not sure if everyone would appreciate that technological advancement as it may lead to complications for those who are not tech savy and just prefer traditional way of controling the rooms like 99% of other hotels.

Overall excellent experience!

reviews.title: Not a pleasant experience in terms of 5 star service but comfortable room
reviews.text:
I had a four night stay at this hotel in total. 

It’s very unfortunate but I have to say I had a terrible check in experience twice in the space of couple of days.:

I do understand you are in a very busy time period but it was very disappointing.

I was given a room with a twin bed stuck together for my first check in.
I was moved to a different room on the same floor but I had to wait for hours.

For my second check in I was given a higher floor room but was informed the room was not ready yet and will be ready in 30 minutes. I came back later in the day to find I was given a much lower floor room and had a sudden room change because something was “broken” in the room.


 I expect a much better service from a five star hotel chain overall.

reviews.title: An average of 5-star hotel in reasonable price
reviews.text: Sofitel Sukhumvit is in good location: 5-10 min walk to Asok/Nana station with lots of coffee shop, restaurants, bars, convenience store, supermarket nearby. 7-11 is located just on the right side of the hotel and a coffee shop is located on the left side of the hotel with 3 min walk and both are open 24 hours. The pool is good, with lots of sunshine enjoyable (many pools in hotels of Thailand are shaded by surrounding buildings!) and delicious food. But the pool is quite small and benches may be full in peak hours.
reviews.positives: Good location, swimming pool, reasonable price

reviews.title: Luxury Staycation
reviews.text: We have enjoyed our stay in Sofitel Sukhumvit. We were treated as VIPs from the moment we check-in until check-out. It was our anniversary and they surprise us with honeymoon set-up in our room. We stayed in the Club room and enjoyed the benefits of evening cocktails and exclusive breakfast in Club Millesime. Room very modern and clean and super soft and comfortable bed. Definitely one of the best hotels we have stayed in!

reviews.title: Excellent choice in the middle of Sukhumvit
reviews.text:
after 8 days in Club Millesime, I was sad to leave. The club is very good with a very nice food and drinks selction for afternoon tea and evening. The club gets a 10, including a superb staff performance.
However, there were some issues in the room, but complaining on high level: there were some visible traces of usage which could be repaired, wifi was sometimes not more than a lame duck and houskeeping inconsistent.
I will definitiely stay in Club Millesime again if my location has to be Sukhumvit.

reviews.title: Beautiful hotel but the staff dropped the ball
reviews.text: This is a beautiful hotel but on arrival I was given a key to the wrong room two times and I was staying on the 28th floor so I went on several elevator rides to try to get into my room. On the third attempt  to gain access to my room I had a manager follow me to make sure I could get into my room. Again the key didn't work. I waited outside my room for another 15-20 minutes waiting for manager with an all room access key to get me in. While I was waiting with another manager, she was very apologetic and I was ok with the situation but she told me that she would do something nice from the hotel as an apology. I thought this was a nice gesture but nothing ever came of it. No gift basket, no free drink, nothing. I asked about it later and again they apologized and said they would talk with the GM but still nothing came of it. I have stayed at this hotel before and enjoyed it very much. To me what happened and how it was handled fell short of 5 star. I will say that I enjoy this property very much and will most likely stay there again. I'm in BKK several times a year and I have stayed in most of the 5 star hotels in this area and Sofitel has been my first choice for the last year but now I will consider other hotels when I am booking my stay in BKK.
